3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"math/rand"
6		"strings"
7		"sync"
8		"testing"
9		"unicode"
10	
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
13	)

15	// TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety spawns 10 goroutines each creating 10 beads
16	// against the same store. Verifies all 100 beads exist, no duplicates, no corruption.
17	func TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety(t *testing.T) {
18		t.Parallel()
19		s := newTestStore(t)
20	
21		const goroutines = 10
22		const beadsEach = 10
23		const total = goroutines * beadsEach
24	
25		var wg sync.WaitGroup
26		errCh := make(chan error, total)
27	
28		for g := 0; g < goroutines; g++ {
29			g := g
30			wg.Add(1)
31			go func() {
32				defer wg.Done()
33				for i := 0; i < beadsEach; i++ {
34					b := &Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("goroutine-%d-bead-%d", g, i)}
35					if err := s.Create(b); err != nil {
36						errCh <- fmt.Errorf("goroutine %d create %d: %w", g, i, err)
37					}
38				}
39			}()
40		}
41	
42		wg.Wait()
43		close(errCh)
44	
45		var errs []error
46		for err := range errCh {
47			errs = append(errs, err)
48		}
49		require.Empty(t, errs, "concurrent creates must not error: %v", errs)
50	
51		beads, err := s.ReadAll()
52		require.NoError(t, err)
53		assert.Len(t, beads, total, "all %d beads must be present", total)
54	
55		// Check for duplicates
56		seen := make(map[string]int)
57		for _, b := range beads {
58			seen[b.ID]++
59		}
60		for id, count := range seen {
61			assert.Equal(t, 1, count, "bead %s appears %d times (duplicate)", id, count)
62		}
63	
64		// Check no corruption: every bead must have a valid ID, title, and status
65		for _, b := range beads {
66			assert.NotEmpty(t, b.ID, "bead ID must not be empty")
67			assert.NotEmpty(t, b.Title, "bead title must not be empty")
68			assert.Contains(t, []string{StatusOpen, StatusInProgress, StatusClosed}, b.Status,
69				"bead %s has invalid status %q", b.ID, b.Status)
70		}
71	}
72	
73	// TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions spawns 5 goroutines each cycling a single
74	// bead through open→in_progress→closed→open. After all iterations the bead
75	// must have a valid status and the JSONL file must be fully parseable.
76	func TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions(t *testing.T) {
77		t.Parallel()
78		s := newTestStore(t)
79	
80		b := &Bead{Title: "status-churn"}
81		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
82		id := b.ID
83	
84		const goroutines = 5
85		const iterations = 20
86	
87		statuses := []string{StatusOpen, StatusInProgress, StatusClosed}
88		var wg sync.WaitGroup
89	
90		for g := 0; g < goroutines; g++ {
91			g := g
92			wg.Add(1)
93			go func() {
94				defer wg.Done()
95				for i := 0; i < iterations; i++ {
96					next := statuses[(g+i)%len(statuses)]
97					// Ignore errors: contention may cause transient failures that are
98					// acceptable (e.g. attempting to set a status that is already set).
99					// The critical invariant is no corruption.
100					_ = s.Update(id, func(bead *Bead) {
101						bead.Status = next
102					})
103				}
104			}()
105		}
106	
107		wg.Wait()
108	
109		// Bead must still exist with a valid status
110		got, err := s.Get(id)
111		require.NoError(t, err, "bead must be readable after concurrent status updates")
112		assert.Contains(t, []string{StatusOpen, StatusInProgress, StatusClosed}, got.Status,
113			"bead must have a valid status after concurrent updates")
114	
115		// JSONL file must be parseable end-to-end
116		allBeads, err := s.ReadAll()
117		require.NoError(t, err, "JSONL file must be parseable after concurrent status transitions")
118		assert.NotEmpty(t, allBeads, "store must not be empty after updates")
119	
120		// Verify no partial writes: every line must decode cleanly
121		for _, bead := range allBeads {
122			assert.NotEmpty(t, bead.ID)
123			assert.NotEmpty(t, bead.Title)
124		}
125	}
126	
127	// TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend reproduces the reported bug:
128	// one goroutine closes a bead while another creates a new bead concurrently.
129	// Both operations must persist; neither must clobber the other.
130	func TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend(t *testing.T) {
131		t.Parallel()
132		s := newTestStore(t)
133	
134		// Pre-create the bead that will be closed
135		existing := &Bead{Title: "to-be-closed"}
136		require.NoError(t, s.Create(existing))
137		closeID := existing.ID
138	
139		const rounds = 50
140	
141		for round := 0; round < rounds; round++ {
142			// Re-open the bead so we can close it again
143			require.NoError(t, s.Update(closeID, func(b *Bead) {
144				b.Status = StatusOpen
145			}))
146	
147			newTitle := fmt.Sprintf("new-bead-round-%d", round)
148			var newID string
149	
150			var wg sync.WaitGroup
151			wg.Add(2)
152	
153			// Goroutine A: close the existing bead
154			go func() {
155				defer wg.Done()
156				_ = s.Close(closeID)
157			}()
158	
159			// Goroutine B: create a new bead
160			go func() {
161				defer wg.Done()
162				nb := &Bead{Title: newTitle}
163				if err := s.Create(nb); err == nil {
164					newID = nb.ID
165				}
166			}()
167	
168			wg.Wait()
169	
170			// The new bead must exist
171			if newID != "" {
172				got, err := s.Get(newID)
173				require.NoError(t, err, "round %d: new bead %s must exist", round, newID)
174				assert.Equal(t, newTitle, got.Title, "round %d: new bead title must match", round)
175			}
176	
177			// The closed bead's status must reflect the close (it may have been
178			// re-opened by a subsequent round, but within this round, if the close
179			// completed first and create ran after, the close must be visible).
180			// The critical check: the closed bead must still exist and be parseable.
181			closedBead, err := s.Get(closeID)
182			require.NoError(t, err, "round %d: closed bead must still exist", round)
183			assert.NotEmpty(t, closedBead.ID, "round %d: closed bead ID must not be empty", round)
184		}
185	}
186	
187	// TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost is the strict regression test for the bug:
188	// close must not be lost when a concurrent create happens after the close acquires
189	// the lock but before it writes. With the directory-lock implementation, the lock
190	// serializes these operations, so the closed status must always be visible after
191	// both goroutines complete (if the close finishes last, the status is closed;
192	// if create finishes last, the store was read AFTER the close, so closed is preserved).
193	func TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost(t *testing.T) {
194		t.Parallel()
195		s := newTestStore(t)
196	
197		existing := &Bead{Title: "must-stay-closed"}
198		require.NoError(t, s.Create(existing))
199		closeID := existing.ID
200	
201		const rounds = 20
202	
203		for round := 0; round < rounds; round++ {
204			// Reset to open
205			require.NoError(t, s.Update(closeID, func(b *Bead) {
206				b.Status = StatusOpen
207			}))
208	
209			// Run close and create concurrently, then wait for both
210			var wg sync.WaitGroup
211			wg.Add(2)
212	
213			closeErr := make(chan error, 1)
214			createErr := make(chan error, 1)
215			createdID := make(chan string, 1)
216	
217			go func() {
218				defer wg.Done()
219				closeErr <- s.Close(closeID)
220			}()
221	
222			go func() {
223				defer wg.Done()
224				nb := &Bead{Title: fmt.Sprintf("concurrent-create-%d", round)}
225				err := s.Create(nb)
226				createErr <- err
227				if err == nil {
228					createdID <- nb.ID
229				} else {
230					createdID <- ""
231				}
232			}()
233	
234			wg.Wait()
235	
236			cErr := <-closeErr
237			nErr := <-createErr
238			nID := <-createdID
239	
240			require.NoError(t, cErr, "round %d: close must not error", round)
241			require.NoError(t, nErr, "round %d: create must not error", round)
242	
243			// The closed bead must be closed: since the lock serializes operations,
244			// the create (which reads the file under the lock) must see the post-close state
245			// IF close ran first, OR the close (which reads under the lock) must complete
246			// after create without losing the create. In either ordering, the final file
247			// must reflect both operations.
248			//
249			// The close must be visible: after both complete, whichever ran last wrote
250			// the file. If close ran last → bead is closed. If create ran last → create
251			// read the file AFTER close completed (lock was released), so bead is closed.
252			//
253			// This test documents and verifies the invariant: close is never lost.
254			finalBead, err := s.Get(closeID)
255			require.NoError(t, err, "round %d: closed bead must be readable", round)
256			assert.Equal(t, StatusClosed, finalBead.Status,
257				"round %d: bead must be closed after close+create race — "+
258					"if this fails, the create read a stale snapshot before close wrote", round)
259	
260			// The new bead must also exist
261			if nID != "" {
262				_, err := s.Get(nID)
263				assert.NoError(t, err, "round %d: newly created bead %s must exist", round, nID)
264			}
265		}
266	}
267	
268	// TestChaos_JSONLRoundTripIntegrity writes beads with random/unicode/special
269	// data and verifies all fields survive a round-trip through the JSONL store.
270	func TestChaos_JSONLRoundTripIntegrity(t *testing.T) {
271		t.Parallel()
272	
273		rng := rand.New(rand.NewSource(42))
274	
275		type testCase struct {
276			name  string
277			title string
278			desc  string
279			notes string
280		}
281	
282		cases := []testCase{
283			{name: "ascii", title: "Simple ASCII title", desc: "plain description", notes: ""},
284			{name: "unicode", title: "Unicode: 日本語テスト", desc: "emoji: 🚀🎯✅", notes: "混合 content"},
285			{name: "special-chars", title: `Tabs	and "quotes" and \backslash`, desc: "line1\nline2\nline3", notes: ""},
286			{name: "empty-optional", title: "Only title is required", desc: "", notes: ""},
287			{name: "max-length", title: strings.Repeat("A", 200), desc: strings.Repeat("B", 1000), notes: strings.Repeat("C", 500)},
288			{name: "json-injection", title: `{"injected":"json"}`, desc: `"nested":"value"`, notes: `]},{"id":"fake"`},
289			{name: "null-like", title: "null", desc: "false", notes: "0"},
290			{name: "whitespace", title: "  spaces around  ", desc: "\t\ttabbed\t\t", notes: "   "},
291		}
292	
293		// Add randomized cases
294		for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
295			cases = append(cases, testCase{
296				name:  fmt.Sprintf("random-%d", i),
297				title: randomString(rng, 5, 80),
298				desc:  randomString(rng, 0, 200),
299				notes: randomString(rng, 0, 100),
300			})
301		}
302	
303		s := newTestStore(t)
304		created := make([]*Bead, 0, len(cases))
305	
306		for _, tc := range cases {
307			tc := tc
308			b := &Bead{
309				Title:       tc.title,
310				Description: tc.desc,
311				Notes:       tc.notes,
312				Labels:      []string{"chaos", tc.name},
313				Priority:    rng.Intn(5),
314			}
315			if err := s.Create(b); err != nil {
316				// If title is empty after trimming, skip — that's a validation rule, not a bug
317				if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "title is required") {
318					t.Logf("skipping case %q: title trims to empty", tc.name)
319					continue
320				}
321				t.Fatalf("case %q: create failed: %v", tc.name, err)
322			}
323			created = append(created, b)
324		}
325	
326		// Read all back and verify field-by-field
327		beads, err := s.ReadAll()
328		require.NoError(t, err, "ReadAll must succeed after writing random beads")
329	
330		byID := make(map[string]Bead, len(beads))
331		for _, b := range beads {
332			byID[b.ID] = b
333		}
334	
335		for _, orig := range created {
336			got, ok := byID[orig.ID]
337			if !assert.True(t, ok, "bead %s must survive round-trip", orig.ID) {
338				continue
339			}
340			assert.Equal(t, orig.Title, got.Title, "bead %s: Title mismatch", orig.ID)
341			assert.Equal(t, orig.Description, got.Description, "bead %s: Description mismatch", orig.ID)
342			assert.Equal(t, orig.Notes, got.Notes, "bead %s: Notes mismatch", orig.ID)
343			assert.Equal(t, orig.Priority, got.Priority, "bead %s: Priority mismatch", orig.ID)
344			assert.Equal(t, orig.Status, got.Status, "bead %s: Status mismatch", orig.ID)
345			assert.ElementsMatch(t, orig.Labels, got.Labels, "bead %s: Labels mismatch", orig.ID)
346		}
347	}
348	
349	// randomString generates a random string with printable characters including
350	// some unicode, in the range [minLen, maxLen) characters.
351	func randomString(rng *rand.Rand, minLen, maxLen int) string {
352		if maxLen <= minLen {
353			return ""
354		}
355		length := minLen + rng.Intn(maxLen-minLen)
356		if length == 0 {
357			return ""
358		}
359	
360		// Character pools
361		pools := []string{
362			"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789",
363			` !@#$%^&*()_+-=[]{}|;':\",./<>?`,
364			"αβγδεζηθικλμνξοπρστυφχψω",
365			"日本語中文한국어",
366			"🚀🎯✅❌💡🔧",
367		}
368	
369		var sb strings.Builder
370		for i := 0; i < length; i++ {
371			pool := pools[rng.Intn(len(pools))]
372			runes := []rune(pool)
373			r := runes[rng.Intn(len(runes))]
374			// Skip control characters that would break JSONL lines
375			if r == '\n' || r == '\r' || r == 0 || !unicode.IsPrint(r) {
376				r = 'X'
377			}
378			sb.WriteRune(r)
379		}
380		return sb.String()
381	}
382

1	package bead
2	
3	// Backend defines the storage interface for beads.
4	// The JSONL backend is the default. The bd and br backends shell out
5	// to external tools with the same bead-compatible interface.
6	type Backend interface {
7		Init() error
8		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
9		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
10		WithLock(fn func() error) error
11	}
12	
13	// BackendType constants
14	const (
15		BackendJSONL = "jsonl"
16		BackendBD    = "bd"
17		BackendBR    = "br"
18	)
19	
20	const DefaultCollection = "beads"
21

what is blocking:

  Without the axon Backend implementation, there is no target API to

  must call `CreateEntity`, `CreateLink`, and `CommitTransaction`
  against the axon backend; none of those entry points exist in the

    (a) inventing the axon backend in this same pass — out of scope,

    (b) writing the migrator against a stub interface that will be

        verified end-to-end, and AC #5 (integration test running
        migration on a fixture repo and asserting equivalence) is
        not satisfiable without a real backend to round-trip against.

  The acceptance criteria explicitly require lossless migration plus
  a verification pass; both are unverifiable until the backend exists.

  close first. Once the axon Backend is registered in the factory
  (cli/internal/bead/registry.go) and exposes the per-entity surface
  described in TD-030 §"Backend interface (Go)" — Create / Get /
  Update / AppendEvent / AddDep / Close / Export / Import — this bead
  becomes straightforward: extend cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go with a
  `--to=axon` flag, iterate beads.jsonl + beads-archive.jsonl + any
  per-bead .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl sidecars, drive the
  axon Backend per the TD-030 Migration plan, and add a verification
  pass (count parity + sampled hash comparison). Idempotency falls
  out of OCC presence-checks as TD-030 already specifies.

  The bead's dependency graph should be enforced in the queue: this
  bead should not become eligible until ddx-95ec5ed5 is closed. If
  the dependency was not declared via `ddx bead dep add` on this
  bead's id (only the textual description names it), that link

reason: |
  Bead ddx-c9ef6c4f ("Implement axon backend behind selectable backend flag")
  cannot be completed in a single execute-bead pass because two upstream
  prerequisites are not yet in place, and the AC as written requires a
  working end-to-end integration against an external Rust service. Forcing
  a commit here would land non-functional scaffolding (the failure mode
  the parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was already BLOCKED for).

  Specific gaps observed in the current worktree (base ab0a056a):

  1. Dependency bead ddx-bbdd7564 ("Introduce selectable bead Backend
     interface in cli/internal/bead") is still status=open. The current
     cli/internal/bead/backend.go defines only the legacy interface:

         Backend interface {
             Init() error
             ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
             WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
             WithLock(fn func() error) error
         }

     TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md
     §"Backend interface (Go)") explicitly says this interface is "too
     coarse for Axon — WriteAll(beads []Bead) cannot reasonably be
     implemented against a remote entity store" and prescribes a richer
     per-entity surface (Get/Create/Update with OCC Version,
     AppendEvent, AddDep/RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export/Import,
     ErrVersionMismatch, etc.). Implementing the axon backend against
     the legacy 4-method interface would (a) violate TD-030 and (b)
     produce a backend that cannot honor claim/OCC semantics that the
     bead description and AC #3 require ("plus claim semantics and
     event append"). This bead's <description> says it "follows the TD
     authored in the design child" — that TD assumes the interface
     refactor has landed first.

  2. No Axon Go SDK or generated gRPC client exists in this repo.
     /home/erik/Projects/axon ships axon.proto at
     crates/axon-server/proto/axon.proto (377 lines, AxonService with
     CreateEntity/GetEntity/UpdateEntity/CommitTransaction/Traverse/
     QueryEntities/PutSchema/CreateCollection/etc.) but no Go bindings.
     TD-030 §"Wire transport" specifies generation via
     protoc-gen-go + protoc-gen-go-grpc into cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/
     with a `make gen-axon-client` target and a checked-in axon.proto
     plus sha256. None of that scaffolding exists; protoc toolchain
     setup, proto vendoring, and the make target are themselves a
     non-trivial chunk of work that should be its own bead.

  3. AC #5 ("ddx bead commands work end-to-end against the axon
     backend on a fresh repo, no migration required") cannot be
     verified inside this worktree. Per TD-030 §D1 the operator runs a
     separate axon-server process; per §D4 the backend refuses all ops
     when Axon is unreachable. There is no Axon server running in this
     execution environment and no fixture / test-double that the AC
     would accept ("end-to-end ... on a fresh repo" rules out an
     in-memory fake).

  Additional context: the parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was BLOCKED in
  post-merge review on 2026-05-02 for landing only execution evidence
  with no implementation, then decomposed into seven children. This
  bead is one of those children but was queued out of dependency order
  — ddx-bbdd7564 (the interface refactor child) needs to land first.

what_is_done:
  - Read TD-030 (the design child's output) end-to-end.
  - Confirmed current Backend interface in cli/internal/bead/backend.go
    is the legacy form, not the TD-030 form.
  - Confirmed dependency bead ddx-bbdd7564 is still open.
  - Confirmed no axon proto/client is vendored under cli/internal/bead/.

what_is_blocking:
  - ddx-bbdd7564 must land first to introduce the per-entity Backend
    interface (Get/Create/Update with Version/OCC, AppendEvent,
    AddDep/RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export/Import, error sentinels)
    that this bead's axon implementation needs to satisfy.
  - A separate bead should establish the gRPC client generation
    pipeline: vendor cli/internal/bead/axon/proto/axon.proto from the
    axon repo (with sha256 pin), add the make gen-axon-client target,
    add tooling docs for protoc-gen-go / protoc-gen-go-grpc, and
    commit the generated cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/ tree. This is
    explicitly called out by TD-030 §"Wire transport" but is not in
    the current bead's scope and has no existing bead covering it.
  - AC #5 needs a clarified verification path: either (a) require a
    running Axon test server in CI (with the deployment work that
    implies), or (b) loosen "end-to-end" to mean "round-trip against
    a vendored Axon test harness" and add the harness as a bead.

what_a_followup_would_need:
  - Re-queue this bead (ddx-c9ef6c4f) only after ddx-bbdd7564 closes
    with the TD-030-shaped Backend interface in place.
  - Add (and close) a new prerequisite bead for the axon gRPC client
    generation: vendor proto, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit
    generated stubs under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.
  - Add a new prerequisite bead for the Axon test harness used by AC
    #5 (either a test-only docker-compose entry, a `go test`-managed
    server-under-test, or an explicit "skip if AXON_ADDR unset"
    integration-test gate per the project's existing test-tier
    conventions).
  - Then this bead becomes a focused implementation: wrap the
    generated client in cli/internal/bead/axon/, register the
    "axon" backend in cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plumb the
    DDX_BEAD_BACKEND / .ddx/config.yaml selector, and add the
    create/claim/close/dep unit tests AC #4 names (against the
    harness from the new prerequisite bead).",

input.content:
status: blocked_by_dependency

what is done:
  - Read cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go end-to-end (5 chaos tests:
    ConcurrentAppendSafety, AtomicStatusTransitions,
    ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, ConcurrentCloseNotLost,
    JSONLRoundTripIntegrity).
  - Inspected the current Backend interface in
    cli/internal/bead/backend.go: still the legacy 4-method shape
    (Init / ReadAll / WriteAll / WithLock) with constants only for
    BackendJSONL, BackendBD, BackendBR. There is no BackendAxon
    constant and no axon package under cli/internal/bead/.
  - Inspected dependency status in .ddx/beads.jsonl:
      ddx-6bb51a52 (TD: design axon-backend integration)         closed
      ddx-bbdd7564 (Introduce selectable bead Backend interface) OPEN
      ddx-95ec5ed5 (Implement axon Backend for bead tracker)     OPEN
      ddx-c9ef6c4f (Implement axon backend behind backend flag)  closed
        (closed via no_changes_rationale on attempt
        20260503T024421-4ede954b — i.e. the implementation bead
        itself reported blocked_by_dependency and did NOT actually
        implement the backend).
  - Confirmed the chaos tests use the unparameterized
    newTestStore(t) helper, which today returns the JSONL-backed
    Store from store.go. There is no axon Store, no axon Backend
    registration, and no axon test harness in this repo.

what is blocking:
  This bead's acceptance criteria require running the chaos suite
  *against the axon backend* and asserting multi-worker fairness,
  claim correctness under contention, and crash/recovery semantics.
  None of that is verifiable today because:

  1. The axon Backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5) is open. There
     is no axon/ subpackage, no generated gRPC client under
     cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/, and no factory entry that maps
     "axon" to a Store. Parameterizing chaos_test.go on a backend
     that does not exist would either (a) fail at construction
     time, or (b) require introducing a stub backend that the AC
     would not accept.

  2. The selectable Backend interface refactor (ddx-bbdd7564) is
     also open. TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/
     TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md §"Backend interface (Go)")
     explicitly says the current 4-method interface is too coarse
     for axon and must be replaced with a per-entity surface
     (Get / Create / Update with OCC Version, AppendEvent,
     AddDep / RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export / Import,
     ErrVersionMismatch). Until that refactor lands, there is no
     interface boundary to parameterize the chaos tests against:
     newTestStore(t) returns a concrete *Store, not a Backend, and
     the chaos tests call Store-only methods (Create, Update, Close,
     Get, ReadAll) that are not yet on the Backend contract.

  3. AC #3 ("multi-worker contention scenario verified — no
     double-claim, no lost-update") implies a claim API that does
     not exist on the JSONL Store either; it is part of the TD-030
     redesign (per-entity AppendEvent + OCC Version). Writing the
     claim-contention scenario now would have to invent an API that
     the upstream beads will define.

  4. AC #4 ("CI runs the suite for both backends") requires a
     running axon-server (per TD-030 §D1 / §D4 the axon backend
     refuses all ops when axon is unreachable). No such server is
     available in this execution environment, and there is no
     vendored test harness or "skip if AXON_ADDR unset" gate yet.

  Implementing this bead now would require either:
    (a) reproducing the axon backend, the interface refactor, the
        gRPC client generation, and a test harness in a single
        execute-bead pass — far outside the scope this bead was
        sized for, and explicitly the work of three other beads;
    (b) committing red or stub code (parameterized harness
        targeting a non-existent backend) — which the execute-bead
        contract forbids ("Do not commit partial, exploratory, or
        red code hoping the reviewer will accept it").

what a follow-up attempt would need:
  - ddx-bbdd7564 closes with the TD-030-shaped per-entity Backend
    interface, and chaos_test.go's newTestStore is refactored to
    return that Backend (or a thin adapter exposing the methods the
    chaos tests need: Create, Update, Close, Get, ReadAll, plus the
    new claim/append-event surface AC #3 references).
  - ddx-95ec5ed5 closes with a real axon Backend registered in
    cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plus a test harness AC #4 can
    rely on (either a docker-compose axon-server entry, a
    `go test`-managed server-under-test, or an explicit
    "skip if AXON_ADDR unset" gate consistent with the project's
    test-tier conventions).
  - A prerequisite bead (not yet filed, as far as I can see) for
    the axon gRPC client generation: vendor axon.proto with sha256
    pin, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit the generated tree
    under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.
  - Then this bead becomes focused: parameterize newTestStore on a
    backend factory (table-driven sub-tests for "jsonl" and "axon"
    using the harness above), add the multi-worker claim/contention
    scenario the original chaos suite does not yet cover (AC #3),
    add a CI matrix entry that runs the suite against both
    backends (AC #4), and fix any axon-side issues that the new
    contention scenario surfaces.

  Recommendation for the queue: enforce the dep graph in `ddx bead
  ready` so this bead does not become eligible until ddx-bbdd7564
  AND ddx-95ec5ed5 are both closed. The textual "Depends on …"
  language in the description is not enough; a concrete
  `ddx bead dep add ddx-dfb1c6d7 ddx-95ec5ed5` (and same for
  ddx-bbdd7564) would prevent further premature dispatches that
  burn cost on no_changes_rationale outcomes.

data.text: Bead is blocked by upstream deps. The axon Backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5) and the selectable Backend interface refactor (ddx-bbdd7564) are both still open — there is no axon package, no gRPC client, and no test harness in the tree to run chaos_test.go against. I wrote the rationale to `.ddx/executions/20260503T024757-3b9a4e9e/no_changes_rationale.txt` and made no code changes.
